text:
Market today: Macro is murky - oil wobbling on Iran deal breakdown, LNG disruption, and Japan spending $74B defending the yen against the Fed. None of that is a direct tech catalyst, but yen/dollar tension and energy noise can rattle risk appetite fast. Your chip-heavy book is riding a calm tape right now, not a ripping one.

Your holdings: All scanner statuses show HOLD or NEW - nothing screaming sell today. That said, SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged chip ETF that decays daily and can cut in half on a bad week - margin amplifies that pain hard. SPAL is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged ETF (+1.91%) - treat it as a lit fuse, not a position. QQQI (+0.67%) is nearly flat; confirm it's pulling its weight as your income anchor.

Watch: (1) NVDA price action - it's the gravitational center of your entire sector. (2) Dollar/yen moves - if yen keeps spiking, risk-off can hit tech quickly.

Bottom line: No fires today, but your two leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) on margin are where a bad day gets ugly fast - know your exits cold.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - that's a 25% drop from here. On margin, that's brutal. Consider tightening your stop or trimming size if you're not comfortable with that full drawdown.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, moves fast, decays - if you haven't set a hard stop at $21.38, do it now. Small position discipline is the whole game here.
No new buys today - scanner has setups but no headline catalyst strong enough to justify adding risk to an already leveraged, concentrated book.
